title: Connect and Operate MCP in Codex and Claude · Protocol Repair Bay
summary: Configure one safe server in both Codex and Claude Code. Learners isolate plausible failures, compare competing hypotheses, and record cause-level repair evidence. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in cross-client MCP configuration and smoke tests and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Configure scoped Codex STDIO or HTTP servers, timeouts, tools, approvals, and authentication.
Configure Claude Code local, project, or user MCP scope and review shared server trust.
Verify initialization, primitive discovery, tool calls, errors, and shutdown in each client.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""Local MCP protocol and tool-contract portfolio."""

steps.title: Implement the cause-level repair
steps.instruction: Append a agent-ext-support-runbook-nwlav4: manifest entry with concrete verify plus failure, owner, or repair evidence.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Support Runbook into observable performance evidence.

content.code:
agent-ext-support-runbook-nwlav4:
  verify: [replace with a deterministic check]
  failure: [replace with the rejected condition]
  repair: [replace with a local reproduction and correction path]
content.caption: Shape of the required increment; replace generic language with the current stakeholder case.

content.text: The Support Runbook structural check is only one gate. cross-client MCP configuration and smoke tests must also survive the stated changed case and causal explanation.
